channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Cargo parametric insurer charts route to US$68bn protection gap with ‘new shiny object’
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Hyland expands AI content platform across Asia-Pacific
item.description: Hyland expanded its AI-native Content Innovation Cloud across the Asia-Pacific region through its partnership with Amazon Web Services. The expansion brings Hyland’s platform to the AWS Asia Pacific (Sydney) Region, allowing organizations in regulated industries including insurance, healthcare, financial services, education, and government to deploy AI-driven content management capabilities while meeting local data residency requirements. The platform is designed to support “agentic enterprise” operations by combining enterprise content management, AI orchestration, and automation tools.

item.title: Boston Mutual Life appoints EVP of distribution
item.description: Boston Mutual Life Insurance appointed Jack Douglas as Executive Vice President of Distribution, effective June 8, 2026. Boston Mutual Life said its distribution team focuses on workplace insurance products including life, accident, disability, and critical illness coverage, serving customers such as union members, public sector employees, first responders, teachers, and small businesses through broker partnerships. He joins Boston Mutual Life from Assurity, where he most recently served as Executive Vice President of Worksite Sales.

item.title: Integrity da el salto: así será su estrategia para conquistar el crédito empresarial en Colombia
item.description: El neobanco Integrity, que llegó a Colombia hace dos años , obtuvo la autorización de la Superintendencia Financiera para operar como compañía de financiamiento , una licencia que activará en el segundo semestre de este año. Con ello, las pequeñas y medianas empresas podrán acceder a los servicios de factoring , confirming , working capital y crédito bilateral o corporativo. “Nos enfocaremos en compañías pequeñas y medianas en crecimiento, especialmente aquellas que están en una etapa en la que requieren soluciones más sofisticadas de liquidez, capital de trabajo y acompañamiento financiero, pero que muchas veces no encuentran una oferta suficientemente flexible o especializada”, dijo David Rey, CEO de Integrity Corporación Financiera.

item.title: The Iran War Is Pushing the Global Gas Trade into the Shadows
item.description: “You look at all those chokepoints, and all of a sudden the foundation of seaborne international global trade just goes out the window. The Al Rayyan, loaded with Qatari LNG, tailed another gas carrier coming from the emirate, the Fuwairit, which was preparing to cross the waterway under a deal between Pakistan and Iran. When US and Israeli strikes on Iran began in late February, Qatar was among the first producers to find itself caught in the economic crossfire.

item.title: 100 Days of Summer Teen Driving Safety Campaign Begins
item.description: This summer, AAA is urging drivers to commit to “100 Days of Safe Driving” between Memorial Day and Labor Day. “The ‘100 Days of Safe Driving’ campaign is about encouraging drivers to make simple choices behind the wheel that can help save lives,” said Gene Boehm, president and CEO of AAA. According to its review of national crash data, more than 30% of fatal crashes involving teen drivers and nearly one-third of all impaired-driving fatalities occur between Memorial Day and Labor Day.

item.title: USQRisk appoints Chris Giuffre as President following leadership changes
item.description: USQRisk, a managing general underwriter within Ryan Alternative Risk, a division of Ryan Specialty, has announced the promotion of Chris Giuffre to President of USQRisk with immediate effect. Chris’ experience developing unique programs and convergence products align with our growth strategy to affirm our position as a leading alternative risk transfer underwriter.” Giuffre added: “I’m honoured to step into this expanded role as President of USQRisk. The company noted that following this change, Michael Zipper will step away from the President role but will continue in his position as Co-CEO of USQRisk alongside Parag Abvishi, Co-CEO and Chief Underwriting Officer.

item.title: Business Moves: WTW Buys Redefind to Boost Access to Crypto and Digital Asset Insurance Offering; ASR Agrees to Strategic Investment From PE Firm Vitruvian Partners
item.description: WTW Buys Redefind to Boost Access to Crypto, Digital Asset Insurance Insurance broker WTW announced it has acquisition of Redefind , an end-to-end web-based platform, designed to facilitate access to insurance products for crypto and digital assets. WTW said this investment reflects its long-term strategy to expand into next-generation protection solutions for clients exposed to digital finance, crypto ecosystems and tokenized asset environments. The proposition launches as a non-custodial, cost-of-recovery insurance solution, intended to support digital asset owners in the event of theft or loss.

item.title: Sentry to Sponsor PGA Tournament at Torrey Pines
item.description: Sentry Insurance will take over as the sponsor of the PGA Tour event at Torrey Pines Golf Course in San Diego, Calif. The PGA, meanwhile, needed a new sponsor for its Torrey Pines tournament after Farmers Insurance chose not to renew its contract as lead sponsor. “For 75 years the PGA TOUR has hosted elite competition in San Diego, including the last 60 at revered Torrey Pines, and we are proud to build upon that legacy and longtime philanthropic impact with our partners at Sentry Insurance,” said PGA TOUR CEO Brian Rolapp.

item.title: Northeast States Sue Trump Administration Over Offshore Wind Farm Cancellation
item.description: states led by New York sued the Trump administration on Tuesday for canceling a major offshore wind lease off the coast of New York in exchange for a pledge by its owner to invest instead in fossil fuel projects. Department of the Interior to cancel a lease by a subsidiary of France’s TotalEnergies, “reimburse” $795 million to the company, and extract a pledge from the company not to develop new offshore wind projects in the United States. offshore wind projects, which President Donald Trump has said he finds ugly and expensive.

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Complicates Winery Insurance
item.description: Smoke taint claims turn on whether wineries can document when grapes were exposed during increasingly overlapping wildfire and harvest seasons. A vineyard or winery may avoid direct fire damage and still face critical economic loss if smoke exposure affects grape quality during harvest operations or while harvested fruit is awaiting processing. As a result, smoke exposure is becoming a growing risk issue not only for viticulture and winemaking teams but also for insurance brokers, adjusters, and coverage counsel evaluating when a claimed loss occurred and how the condition of the product can be established.

item.title: Report: U.S. States' Fiscal Outlook Is Stable
item.description: But Conning's latest State of the States report finds greater divergence as post-pandemic momentum fades and structural regional differences deepen. Conning maintains a stable outlook for U.S. states in 2026, reflecting continued balance sheet strength and generally prudent fiscal management across, even as operating conditions become more challenging.

item.title: Sentry becomes title sponsor of PGA TOUR event
item.description: Sentry Insurance will become the title sponsor of the PGA TOUR event at Torrey Pines Golf Course in San Diego beginning in 2027, with the tournament renamed “The Sentry.” The event is scheduled to take place Jan. The insurer has previously served as title sponsor of the PGA TOUR’s season-opening event in Hawaii. The competition will air across CBS, Paramount+, Golf Channel, ESPN+, SiriusXM, and PGA TOUR World Feed platforms.

item.title: VIVA Finance adds embedded loan protection from Securian
item.description: Atlanta-based fintech VIVA Finance launched an embedded loan protection offering powered by Walnut Insurance and backed by Securian Financial . Securian Financial will administer the loan payment protection product. The product allows borrowers to add protection for involuntary unemployment, disability, and medical emergencies during the loan origination process.

item.title: Why Cyber Insurance’s “Fire Sale” Won’t Last
item.description: Today the cyber insurance market appears to be having a “fire sale.” Rates are falling, capacity is abundant plus new players continue to enter the market, and buyers are enjoying some of the most affordable coverage in several years. cyber insurance premiums declined for the first time since AM Best began tracking the market in 2015. At the same time, several of the senior cyber insurance execs I speak to are seeing a different kind of exposure emerge with AI: one that looks a lot like an insider risk, but without the traditional controls.

item.title: Report Explores Forces Driving Claim Complexity
item.description: Growth in this category outpaced other injury types, according to the report, which reflects: In auto claims, severity was tied to claim complexity rather than frequency. Insurers and employers face mounting pressure from rising severity, lengthier claim durations and expanding operational demands, a new report shows. Enlyte on Tuesday released its 2026 Envision Trends Report, which reflects growing complexity for the claims space.

item.title: Bus Driver Charged in Deadly Virginia Crash, NTSB Investigating
item.description: Dong, 48, of Staten Island, New York, was charged with two counts of involuntary manslaughter, and additional charges were likely, Virginia State Police said. The National Transportation Safety Board is investigating the crash, a months long task separate from the work of state police. A family of four from Massachusetts who were killed when a bus crashed into multiple vehicles in Virginia were traveling to a wedding with a carload of homemade desserts for the celebration.

item.title: Public Adjusting Firm Head, Personal Injury Lawyer Among Many Charged in Massive Insider Trading Scheme
item.description: Thirty people in total have been charged with participating in the decade-long scheme, which prosecutors say netted tens of millions of dollars and was orchestrated by Nourafchan and personal injury attorney Robert Yadgarov. charges that they participated in a decade-long scheme in which attorneys fed an insider trading ring tips about nearly 30 underway mergers. Martin Weinberg, a lawyer for Nicolo Nourafchan, in a statement said his client “asserted his innocence to each allegation at his arraignment today and we intend a vigorous and compelling defense.” According to authorities, the scheme began in 2014 not long after Nicolo Nourafchan graduated from Yale Law School and landed a job at Sidley Austin.

item.title: Mapfre Re names Chaturbhuj Bansal as head of India branch
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Open banking takes next step forward with launch of UK Payments Initiative scheme
item.description: The UK Payments Initiative (UKPI) announcement signals a major step forward for open banking and commercial variable recurring payments (cVRP). We want to see competition between commercial open banking schemes and expect the launch of the first scheme by UKPI to act as a catalyst for other initiatives to emerge. To strengthen this next phase of open banking, we are supporting industry efforts to establish an independent standards-setting body, and – subject to legislation expected to give us new powers – will consult on a long-term regulatory framework by the end of 2026.

item.title: SaaS Is Dead. Long Live SaaS! AI And The End Of The Rationing Of Knowledge Work
item.description: By now, the headline will be familiar to most Crunchbase News readers: SaaS is Dead. But it does not follow that SaaS is dead. It worked for coal engines, it worked for data centers, and it will also work for AI-powered software.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: Defense Startup Funding Hits An All-Time Record As VCs Begin To Eye Exits
item.description: The defense tech sector’s rise has been years in the making Global defense tech funding totaled $1.6 billion in 2020 before climbing to $3.9 billion in 2021, Crunchbase data shows. Still, it’s not the only defense- or military-related startup drawing large sums of funding. A decade ago, defense tech was considered a niche, even controversial corner of venture capital, with few startup investors daring to place bets on companies working with the military.

item.title: Inaugural Buttonwood Re cat bond an important milestone for Mangrove: CEO Weinstein
item.description: Stephen Weinstein, CEO and Founder of Mangrove Property Insurance, commented: “This inaugural catastrophe bond represents an important milestone for Mangrove. (Series 2026-1) issuance, Stephen Weinstein, Chief Executive Officer and Founder of the firm has highlighted how the inaugural catastrophe bond represents “an important milestone” for the company. With Mangrove Property Insurance recently securing its debut catastrophe bond transaction, the $111 million Buttonwood Re Ltd.

item.title: Market conditions suggest alternative capital will increase further: Guy Carpenter’s Rousseau
item.description: As alternative capital continues to grow, it’s currently estimated share of 20% of the global re/insurance industry is largely an underestimation, according to Guy Carpenter’s Laurent Rousseau, who argues that current market conditions would suggest that it will increase further before it stabilises or shrinks. Broker Guy Carpenter recently published a new report that examines the accelerating integration of alternative capital into the global reinsurance market, as well as the implications for capacity, pricing, and market structure. Commenting on the difficulty of forecasting this integration, Laurent Rousseau, CEO of Global Capital & Advisory and EMEA, Guy Carpenter, noted that the industry should prepare for a landscape that is far more fluid than current estimates suggest.

item.title: Reinsurance, retro key to managing catastrophe risks under climate disaster stress tests: S&P
item.description: A recent series of stress tests conducted by S&P Global Ratings on a number of different insurers and reinsurers, to see how they would withstand a 1-in-250-year catastrophe event has showcased the importance that both reinsurance and retrocession bring towards managing catastrophe risks. S&P’s stress test shows that a 1-in-250-year event would be manageable for most insurers, with the largest 50 insurers (by gross exposure) retaining an 18% capital surplus, on average, in the agency’s test, while catastrophe risk accounted for about 70% of the remaining capital surplus after a 1-in-250- year disaster, on average. This dynamic does not appear to be going away–the industry is adjusting to a reality where extreme weather is increasingly common.” Given this, the agency recently stress-tested global insurers, primary insurers and reinsurers, in order to see how they would withstand a 1-in-250-year catastrophe event.

item.title: FutureProof launches AI E&S Program
item.description: FutureProof Technologies, a venture-backed insurtech startup, has launched a new personal lines property insurance program with Bridge Specialty Group designed to provide excess and surplus (E&S) coverage for condo and renters in catastrophe-prone regions across the southeastern United States, including Florida and Texas. Accelerant’s Risk Exchange provides FutureProof with the stable, long-term capacity needed to grow the program with confidence, along with data analytics infrastructure and operational support that allow the MGA to focus on what it does best: underwriting profitably. The partnership combined FutureProof’s AI underwriting technology, which provides instant quotes, with Accelerant’s data-driven risk exchange.

item.title: Gallagher names Repko as Senior Director, Capital Solutions, PEMA
item.description: Insurance broker and risk adviser Gallagher has appointed Laura Repko to the newly created role of Senior Director, Capital Solutions, for its global multidisciplinary Private Equity and M&A practice (PEMA). “By focusing on understanding the financial dynamics driving deals and tailoring insurance-backed solutions to meet the unique needs of lenders and borrowers, Laura will enable clients to optimise their capital structures and alleviate any points of friction.” Repko added, “This is an exciting time to be joining Gallagher, with its focus on building real breadth and depth of advisory and broking expertise to the benefit of lenders and borrowers. In her new role, Repko will be based in London and focus on providing solutions across a range of lending scenarios to support the flow of finance, including credit enhancement, compliance with banking and accounting regulations, working capital financing, and capital event situations.

item.title: Previsico integrates Snapshot Live’s visual monitoring into flood platform
item.description: Previsico, the live flood forecasting insurtech, has partnered with Snapshot Live to integrate live visual monitoring into its Flood Intelligence Platform, enhancing flood risk forecasting. Snapshot Live provides AI-powered remote visual intelligence, helping organisations eliminate operational blind spots in locations where traditional monitoring solutions fall short. Snapshot Live’s solar-powered, off-grid cellular cameras are deployed at flood-prone locations and capture images on a scheduled basis or in response to live alerts, with frequency increasing automatically as forecast risk rises.

item.title: Anthropic Offers Mythos Model Access to 150 Additional Groups
item.description: Anthropic PBC is allowing 150 additional organizations around the world to access Mythos, an artificial intelligence model aimed at finding cybersecurity vulnerabilities that the company has said was too dangerous to make available to the general public. Anthropic plans to announce the increased number of users Tuesday, bringing the total groups with access to about 200. Though Anthropic declined to name the additional participants, it said the group includes companies and nonprofits that produce key programming code for broader use.

item.title: Shipper Escapes $41.9M Award for Man Paralyzed When Lights Fell From Pallet on Him
item.description: When a temporary staffer operating a reach truck made contact with the lights during a picking operation, the lights slid off the pallet onto Cruz. In its opinion dated June 2, the appeals court ruled that the trial court judge and jury wrongly concluded that Signify should have foreseen the injuries that rendered Juan Cruz a paraplegic as a result of its failure to adequately secure the lights to a pallet for shipping. in Hartford, Connecticut where Cruz worked without stretch-wrapping the lights onto the pallet as was its own and standard industry safety practice and part of its agreement with Rexel.

item.title: Amazon’s Ring Sued Over Facial Recognition Feature
item.description: Amazon was sued on Monday by a Virginia resident over what he said were privacy violations after the company’s Ring doorbell cameras at friends and family members’ homes collected and stored images of his face using facial recognition software. “Millions of other Americans passed by a Ring security camera and unknowingly had their facial recognition information collected.” The suit, which seeks unspecified damages for those impacted, is just the latest in a string of controversies around Amazon’s Ring, the unit that makes the eponymous smart doorbells and security systems. The plaintiff, Charles Sigwalt, who is seeking class-action status, sued Amazon in federal court in Seattle alleging a feature known as “Familiar Faces” retains images of passersby without their consent.

item.title: An AI-Driven Jobs Apocalypse? Yeah, Maybe Not
item.description: Even as companies lay off tens of thousands of workers and cite AI-based efficiencies, it's becoming clear that fears of a jobs apocalypse have been overstated. Maybe belay that thought, at least for the time being. Meanwhile, economic data suggests that AI has not led to widespread job cuts, and companies are increasingly reporting great successes by using AI to make employees' jobs easier and to enhance customer service.

item.title: Aviva expands ChatGPT app to life insurance applications
item.description: Aviva has expanded its ChatGPT app to allow customers to obtain an initial life insurance quote. The development builds on Aviva’s ChatGPT launch in April which enables customers to receive a home insurance quote. “Following the launch of our home insurance app on ChatGPT, we’re pleased to extend this capability to life insurance.

item.title: ANV acquires workers’ comp MGA Specialty Comp Insurance Solutions
item.description: ANV Group Holdings acquired Texas-based MGA Specialty Comp Insurance Solutions from Specialty Program Group, part of Hub International. SCIS specializes in hard-to-place monoline workers’ compensation insurance for middle-market businesses and wrote around $250 million in gross written premium in 2025. “Adding SCIS alongside Risico, our existing workers comp business, establishes ANV as a leading provider of middle-market workers comp with diversified underwriting expertise and a go-to option for hard-to-place risks.

item.title: MetLife and Highlights launch soccer-themed digital magazine for kids
item.description: MetLife partnered with Highlights to launch a free soccer-themed digital magazine aimed at helping children build confidence and resilience through educational activities. The magazine includes soccer-themed stories, games, puzzles, and activities focused on teamwork, self-expression, and problem solving. The initiative is tied to MetLife’s broader efforts around education and sports access, including support for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund.

item.title: Munich Re Specialty incorpora a Rubén Galán como suscriptor senior de energías renovables
item.description: Munich Re Specialty Global Markets sigue dando pasos en la constitución de su equipo directivo para España con el nombramiento de Rubén Galán Araujo como suscriptor senior para energías renovables. Cuenta con 19 años de trayectoria en el sector: los últimos 7 en suscripción y los 12 anteriores en ingeniería de riesgos.

item.title: «Los mejores equipos no son los que no fallan sino los que tienen seguridad para hablar y aprender del error»
item.description: Una de las últimas sesiones del Programa de Liderazgo Femenino de #RedEWI ha contado con la participación de Olivia Loewe, Chief Corporate Affairs & Sustainability Officer de Allianz, quien compartió con las alumnas una inspiradora reflexión sobre liderazgo, cultura corporativa, motivación y gestión de equipos en un entorno empresarial en constante transformación.

item.title: Starbucks to Take AI Usage into Account in Tech Workers’ Bonuses
item.description: The bonus structure, which applies to most tech workers, also takes into account other department priorities such as the performance of the order-and-pay function on the Starbucks app, according to the document. is tying most tech workers’ bonuses to how much they’re using artificial intelligence as the coffee chain seeks to improve efficiency. A quarter of bonuses paid to tech employees will be pegged to department-wide goals that include AI adoption, according to an internal document viewed by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Hanwha Aerospace Explosion Kills Five Workers, Lee Orders Probe
item.description: An explosion at Hanwha Aerospace Co.’s manufacturing facility in South Korea on Monday killed five employees, prompting President Lee Jae Myung to order a full investigation. “Hanwha Group and Hanwha Aerospace will do everything possible to deal with the aftermath of the accident,” the statement added. Hanwha Aerospace is South Korea’s largest defense contractor and has been accelerating production at home and expanding its manufacturing footprint in Europe and the US to meet spiraling demand for munitions, missiles and other weapons systems.

item.title: The Field Inspection Gap: A Growing Structural Risk in Claims Handling
item.description: In property/casualty claims, the initial inspection does more than document damage. A gap has emerged between what is observed in the field and how claims decisions are made. When that inspection is incomplete, rushed, or performed without sufficient expertise, the effects rarely remain isolated.

item.title: Toa Re makes changes to Japan, Singapore leadership
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Willis hires Chintan Aashwini in India to bolster Asia M&A practice
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh hires Kazutoyo Kobayashi as senior vice president for credit specialities in Japan
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: HCMA hires Adil Imani from Verisk as VP within Insurance-Linked Securities team
item.description: Howden Capital Markets & Advisory (HCMA), the insurance and reinsurance broker’s investment banking and insurance-linked securities (ILS) specialist unit, has announced the hire of Adil Imani from Verisk, who joins as an ILS focused Vice President. Imani has worked in the Verisk Catastrophe and Risk Solutions business with a focus on catastrophe bonds and insurance-linked securities for more than 6 years and joined the catastrophe modelling specialist in 2013. In 2019, Imani became a Manager in the Insurance-Linked Securities business at Verisk, quickly moving up to become Senior Manager, Director and most recently an Assistant Vice President.

item.title: SEADRIF and FAO launch first parametric drought insurance pilot in Lao PDR
item.description: The SEADRIF Insurance Company and the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) have launched an anticipatory drought insurance pilot in Lao People’s Democratic Republic (Lao PDR), the first time a Southeast Asian government has secured parametric insurance specifically designed to deploy pre-arranged finance ahead of drought impacts. According to SEADRIF, the insurance pilot provides the Ministry of Finance of Lao PDR with access to payouts triggered by early warning data signaling elevated drought risk, before drought majorly affects farming households and rural communities. Benedikt Signer, CEO of the SEADRIF Insurance Company, commented: “Anticipatory action aims to reduce harm before drought becomes a full-scale crisis for communities.

item.title: PFZW allocation target rises for Mt. Logan strategy, shrinks for Munich Re & Swiss Re sidecars
item.description: This comes as two of the private collateralized reinsurance sidecar structures that PFZW has been investing in are reportedly being downsized, resulting in the pension fund’s target allocation ranges for Munich Re and Swiss Re operated sidecars now having shrunk. Now, the latest disclosure from Dutch pension fund for the care and welfare sector PFZW shows that its target investment allocation range for the Mt. The target investment allocation range for the Mt.

item.title: Allied World opens new regional office in DIFC
item.description: Allied World, a global provider of insurance and reinsurance solutions, has opened a new regional office in the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC), marking a significant milestone in the firm’s global growth strategy and strengthening its access to brokers and clients across the Middle East. “Allied World’s presence further strengthens DIFC’s (re)insurance ecosystem and underscores our role as a trusted platform for tier‑one firms looking to serve the Middle East and beyond from Dubai.” Ed Moresco, President, Global Markets, Allied World, added, “Establishing a regional office in Dubai marks a key milestone in our global growth strategy, giving us greater access to brokers and clients throughout the region. His Excellency Arif Amiri, Chief Executive Officer of DIFC Authority, commented, “We are pleased to welcome Allied World to DIFC as they expand their global footprint and establish their regional presence in Dubai.

item.title: AXIS Capital appoints Austin Su as Head of Alternative Risk, NA
item.description: AXIS Capital Holdings Limited, a Bermuda-based global insurer and reinsurer, has announced the appointment of Austin Su as Head of Alternative Risk, North America. Mike McKenna, Head of North America at AXIS, said, “Austin is a proven leader with deep expertise in structured and alternative risk solutions and a strong track record of building high-performing businesses. He will report to Anna Tan, Head of Wholesale Casualty, North America, and focus on building and scaling the company’s alternative risk transfer platform, delivering innovative solutions for complex customer needs, and advancing AXIS’ capabilities in this growing segment.

item.title: Lohmann to lead Artificial Labs European expansion as Head of Europe
item.description: Artificial Labs, a provider of digital broking and underwriting technology for the specialty and commercial insurance markets, has appointed Christopher Lohmann as Head of Europe to lead the company’s strategic growth and expansion across continental Europe. The opportunity to help establish this as a standard approach across European markets is incredibly exciting.” Artificial’s expansion into Europe builds on its established presence in the London Market and follows its recent US expansion, marked by the appointment of Eric Joost to lead its North American strategy. Based in Germany, Lohmann joins Artificial’s executive leadership team, and has been tasked with driving the company’s European strategy.

item.title: Viewpoint: How Climate Change Is Creating Uninsurable Areas Across Europe
item.description: As climate change makes extreme weather events more intense and frequent, “uninsurable areas” are becoming increasingly common. Uninsurable areas refer to places where property insurance has become either impossible to get or to afford. In short, traditional insurance is ill-equipped to confront the reality of climate change.
